title: Card Stack, Grid-Lanes, Drop Caps, @starting-style, Tumbler
description: Five live UI demos: CSS scroll-driven card peel, native masonry grid-lanes, initial-letter drop caps, @starting-style enters, slot-machine digit input.

drops.title: Scroll-Driven Card Stack
drops.description: CSS view() timeline peels a stack of cards away as you scroll — zero JS, pure compositor animation.
drops.concept: Uses animation-timeline: view() with animation-range: exit to tie each card's opacity, scale, and translateY to its own scroll-exit position. The stacking is achieved with position:sticky and graduated top offsets. Fully hardware-accelerated with no main-thread JavaScript.
drops.source: scroll-driven-animations.style demos / CSS Scroll-driven Animations spec

drops.title: CSS Grid-Lanes Masonry
drops.description: Native masonry photo grid using the new display: grid-lanes and grid-template-rows: masonry CSS 2026 properties.
drops.concept: The CSS Grid Level 3 spec settled on grid-lanes as the masonry display mode after years of debate. In 2026 it lands in Safari 26.4+ and Chrome behind a flag. The demo uses progressive enhancement with @supports to layer grid-lanes → masonry fallback → CSS columns fallback.
drops.source: CSS-Tricks / dev.to / CSS Grid Level 3 spec W3C

drops.title: initial-letter Drop Caps
drops.description: Three editorial drop-cap styles — raised, sunken, and animated — using the CSS initial-letter property.
drops.concept: The initial-letter property (now at 91% browser support) handles font metrics, cap-height, and baseline alignment automatically. The two-value form initial-letter: 4 3 creates a 4-line tall letter sunken 3 lines. Combined with @keyframes and @starting-style, caps can animate in on entry.

drops.title: @starting-style Entry Animations
drops.description: Toast, card, and dialog enter animations powered by CSS @starting-style — no JS timing or setTimeout tricks.
drops.concept: @starting-style defines the styles an element has before its first render, letting CSS transitions animate it in. Paired with transition-behavior: allow-discrete and display:none toggling, this pattern replaces the classic JS 'requestAnimationFrame double tick' hack that plagued web developers for years. Baseline Newly Available in 2026.

interaction.click: Trigger toasts, add cards, open dialog

drops.title: Tumbler Digit Input
drops.description: A slot-machine style digit picker with scroll, drag, keyboard, and spring CSS transitions — PIN, time, and quantity variants.
drops.concept: Inspired by iOS-style pickers and Stripe's custom inputs, the tumbler shows a scrollable column of digits with a clip mask, top/bottom fade gradients, and spring cubic-bezier transitions. Each column supports scroll wheel, pointer drag, click arrows, and keyboard arrows/digit keys. Three variants: PIN entry, time picker, and quantity selector.
drops.source: Original concept inspired by Stripe/Apple tumbler/picker UI patterns

interaction.mouse: Scroll wheel or drag up/down each column
